1998-09-01.title: Missile Test by North Korea: Dark Omen for Washington
1998-09-01.abstract: Clinton Administration and arms control experts say North Korea's test of medium-range missile able to reach targets in Japan represents technological breakthrough that has raised new fears of spread of ballistic missiles; with North's record of selling missiles and related technology to Iran, Pakistan and Syria, concern is that more advanced, longer-range missiles are now within reach of less developed countries (M)

1998-09-01.title: North Korea Test-Fires a Missile Off Japan's North
1998-09-01.abstract: SEOUL—  North Korea test-fired its longest-range ballistic missile to date into waters off northern Japan on Monday in a show of strength that analysts believed was designed to impress its own starving people as much as the rest of the world.

1998-09-01.title: I.R.A. Denies Link to Bombing in Northern Ireland and Rejects Calls for Disarmament
1998-09-01.abstract: Irish Republican Army, which has observed cease-fire for 13 months, makes rare public statement denying any connection to recent bombing in Omagh that killed 28 people; rejects demands that it disarm soon and declare end to its war against British sovereignty; statements are seen as attempt to improve its image and help its political wing, as well as to improve image before Pres Clinton's arrival (M)

1998-09-01.title: 53 Years Later, Lawsuit Is Filed on Behalf of Hitler's Slave Labor
1998-09-01.abstract: American and German lawyers, encouraged by success in persuading Swiss banks to repay victims of Holocaust, file class-action suit against Germany's biggest industrial companies on behalf of workers forced into slave labor during Nazi era; suit could lead to payouts by Daimler-Benz, Volkswagen, BMW and Siemens; is biggest attempt yet to force companies entangled in business of Nazi Germany to pay victims 50 years after war (M)

1998-09-01.title: North Korean Missile Is Fired Across Japan
1998-09-01.abstract: North Korea fired a ballistic missile over Japan, which called the firing ''extremely dangerous.''     The action suggests that North Korea has greatly increased the range of its missiles. Before, its missiles could reach only part of Japan, but the new missile appeared to be a medium-range ballistic missile that may be able to reach all of Japan.

1998-09-02.title: Muslims on Trial in France
1998-09-02.abstract: Trial of 138 suspected supporters of Islamic insurgents in Algeria, rounded up in police raids four years ago, starts in prison near Paris (M)

1998-09-02.title: Clinton Tells Moscow Crowd That Future Won't Be Easy
1998-09-02.abstract: Pres Clinton, in meetings with Pres Boris N Yeltsin and in speech to students at Moscow university, delivers sober lecture about hard realities of capitalism and price of international support for relief from Russia's current financial crisis; says there is no easy escape from financial catastrophe that has closed banks, left millions of workers unpaid and caused collapse of Government; officials say Clinton's meetings with Yeltsin were businesslike; only concrete accords to be announced at meetings are agreements to reduce both nations' plutonium stockpiles and to share data on ballistic missile launchings; both were negotiated in weeks leading up to summit meeting; photos (M)

1998-09-02.title: 3 Options for Russia: Populist Path Seems Most Likely but Dangerous
1998-09-02.abstract: Prominent Western economists are considering three possible courses of action for Russia: take populist path of tightening direct control over economy, strike deal with International Monetary Fund or, most radically, link nation's finances to strong foreign currency like dollar; graph; photos (M)

1998-09-02.title: Crisis Batters Emerging Economy, Sending Rand to New Lows
1998-09-02.abstract: Economic crisis in Russia has hit South Africa hard, dealing sharp blow to emerging economy already hurt by Asian crisis; rand, worth about 15 cents, is touching new lows; Russia exports many of same commodities that South Africa does; economists say South Africa is concerned that Russia may sell all it can, driving prices down (M)

1998-09-02.title: Pete Watrous, 82, American Diplomat
1998-09-02.abstract: Livingston Day (Pete) Watrous, career diplomat who specialized in Latin America, dies at age 82 (S)

1998-09-03.title: Malaysian Chief Ousts Deputy Who Opposed Him on Economy
1998-09-03.abstract: Malaysia Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad dismisses his deputy and heir apparent Anwar Ibrahim, who has opposed his insular and free-spending economic policies; Anwar photo (M)

1998-09-03.title: The Firewall: Doubts About a Tactic to Contain Chaos
1998-09-03.abstract: David E Sanger analysis contends that Treasury Dept's 'firewall strategy' to contain spreading outbreaks of financial chaos in foreign countries is coming undone in Indonesia, Malaysia and other Asian countries; notes that under strategey, Treasury and International Monetary Fund would stitch together bailout that insisted on far-reaching reforms, which would rebuild national economies in US image (M)

1998-09-03.title: U.S. Notes Gaps in Data About Drug Plant but Defends Attack; Sudan Envoy Is Angry
1998-09-03.abstract: Defense Sec William S Cohen says that when US launched cruise missiles against factory in Sudan on Aug 20, it was unaware that plant made medicines; says attack was justified by information showing that plant made Empta, key ingredient for VX, nerve agent, and might be linked to Osama bin Laden, Saudi millionaire suspected of ordering bombing of US Embasses in Kenya and Tanzania; Sudan Amb to US M I Mohammed criticizes attack; says plant was not part of Iraqi-backed effort to make weapons for bin Laden; photo (M)

1998-09-03.title: Rongcheng Journal; As Floods Rage, Bitter Choices Over What to Save
1998-09-03.abstract: Rongcheng, China, and other towns along overflowing Yangtze River have suffered some of worst damage of summer's floods; 100,000 people have lost their homes in Jianli County; all of damage in county resulted from decisions to spread swelling waters by breaching inner dikes to relieve pressure on vital main dikes and provincial capital of Wuhan downstream; map; photo (M)

1998-09-03.title: At Third-World Meeting, Hopes for Congo Peace Rise and Fall
1998-09-03.abstract: Hopes for cease-fire in Congo are quickly raised and as quickly dashed at summit meeting of nonaligned nations in Durban, South Africa (M)

1998-09-04.title: Chernomyrdin Rallies Forces for Another Try at Confirmation
1998-09-04.abstract: Acting Prime Min Viktor S Chernomyrdin of Russia rallies new forces to try to stop Communist-led lower house of Parliament from rejecting his nomination for second time; Pres Boris N Yeltsin proposes to reopen talks on sharing power with lower house as constitutional confrontation intensifies and economy threatens to follow currency into free fall; if Chernomyrdin is rejected again, Yeltsin faces politically agonizing choice: resubmitting Chernomyrdin's nomination and then having to disband lower house and call for new parliamentary elections if it is rejected third time, or dropping Chernomyrdin and putting forward compromise candidate; photo (M)

1998-09-04.title: Latin America Tells I.M.F. Economies Are Sound
1998-09-04.abstract: Officials from nine Latin American nations say their economies are basically sound and should not be penalized indiscriminately by investors panicky over financial turmoil in Asia and Russia; officials, in Washington for two-day meeting with International Monetary Fund, say they are not seeking any international financial help, and that they are taking difficult steps needed to weather storm that is threatening to batter much of world; Clinton Administration praises progress of many Latin American countries; officials from Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Uruguay and Venezuela attend meeting (M)

1998-09-04.title: North Koreans May Be Preparing Another Missile Test
1998-09-04.abstract: American and Japanese officials say North Korea may be preparing to test another missile, even as North Korean diplomats meet in New York to discuss their missile and nuclear programs with American officials; preparations detected at North Korean military base suggest that Government of Kim Jong Il is brushing aside international protests over Aug 31 test, which has inflamed tensions in region; Japan has withdrawn offer to begin talks on establishing diplomatic relations with North Korea, and suspended $1 billion payment to Korean Energy Development Organization,international agency building reactors in North Korea in exchange for North Korea's pledge in 1994 to halt construction of nuclear weapons (M)

1998-09-04.title: Minerva Bernardino, 91, Dominican Feminist
1998-09-04.abstract: Minerva Bernardino, major force behind founding of United Nations Commission on Status of Women and a pioneer among Latin American feminists, dies at age 91; photo (M)

1998-09-04.title: Chief U.N. Inspector Reports More Interference by Iraq
1998-09-04.abstract: Chief United Nations weapons inspector for Iraq, Richard Butler, says Government of Saddam Hussein has gone beyond its decision to block surprise inspections and is now interfering with routine monitoring operations; tells Security Council about deteriorating situation in Iraq as United States and Britain move to keep comprehensive sanctions on Iraqis until they resume cooperation; some diplomats say Iraq might not have become so obstructive if Council had rewarded it in July by closing nuclear inspection file and switching to long-term monitoring; some Chinese, Russian and other diplomats also favor review of relations with Iraq and possibly restructuring of United Nations Special Commission, which Butler heads, following Iraqi accusations that Butler and his inspectors overstepped their authority (M)

1998-09-05.title: Ex-Rwandan Premier Gets Life in Prison On Charges of Genocide in '94 Massacres
1998-09-05.abstract: United Nations tribunal sentences Jean Kambanda, former Prime Minister of Rwanda, to life in prison for his part in 1994 genocide there, despite his agreement to plead guilty and testify against other high-ranking officials; he becomes first person in history to be sentenced for the crime of genocide; photo (M)

1998-09-05.title: Reporter's Notebook; The Faces of Clinton: Weary to Somber to Vibrant
1998-09-05.abstract: Emotions shown on Pres Clinton's face and by his gestures swing widely during his visits to Russia and Ireland, from pained confession of his 'profound regret' about the Lewinsky matter to his compassion for the victims of the bombing in Omagh, Northern Ireland; his interactions with Hillary Rodham Clinton lurch from apparent affection to icy distance; overwhelming impression is of a man exhausted and in distress who is able, when necessary, to put aside his private pain and reclaim the brave face of the practice performer; photos (M)

1998-09-05.title: Russians Defer A Vote, Buying Time for Talks
1998-09-05.abstract: Russia's acting Prime Minister, Viktor S Chernomyrdin, lays out his program to pull country out of its economic crisis, but Parliament puts off crucial vote on the new Government until Sept 6, buying more time for political negotiations; Chernomyrdin's plan, presented before he has the mandate to carry it out, gets cool reception from Clinton Administration and International Monetary Fund; bad economic news continues to pile up for Russia, including further sharp drop in ruble, troubles at Inkombank and steep gain in official inflation rate for August; photo (M)

1998-09-05.title: Neither Rain Nor Traffic Dampens Irish Warmth for Clinton
1998-09-05.abstract: Pres Clinton is warmly welcomed to to Dublin, where the Irish thunderously applauded him on his first trip in December 1995 (M)

1998-09-05.title: Massa Marittima Journal; Whatever's in the Lake, It's a Pain and a Puzzle
1998-09-05.abstract: Lago dell'Accesa outside Massa Marittima, Italy, is closed to swimming after a visitor from Germany reports seeing a couple put a crocodile in the lake; the news prompts some Italians to stay away but facinates and draws others; photo; maps (M)

1998-09-06.title: Embassy Bombing Findings
1998-09-06.abstract: Investigators in the embassy bombing in Tanzania say they have learned who transported the bomb and what it was made of. Page 14.

1998-09-06.title: Many Think Kohl's Heir Would Run Better Race
1998-09-06.abstract: Polls that show German Chancellor Helmut Kohl leads Christian Democrats to defeat in forthcoming election show his designated successor, Wolfgang Schauble, would be stronger candidate against Social Democratic leader Gerhard Schroder; Schauble seems uneasy with role but not prepared to challenge mentor, interview; has been paralyzed from waist down since he was shot by assailant in 1990 (M)

1998-09-06.title: Inge Aicher-Scholl, 81, a German Whose Writing Inspired Pacifists
1998-09-06.abstract: Inge Aicher-Scholl, who inspired generation of pacifists in post-World War II Germany by writing about an anti-Nazi youth movement and killing of her brother and sister, dies at 81 (S)

1998-09-06.title: MILITANT LEADER WAS A U.S. TARGET SINCE THE SPRING
1998-09-06.abstract: Clinton Administration officials say Central Intelligence Agency and United States special forces secretly planned raid last spring to seize Osama bin Laden in Afghanistan because of his links to series of anti-American terrorist attacks in recent years; say CIA Dir George Tenet and others shelved plan because of high risks of American and Afghani casualties; says US was working on broad range of other options against bin Laden when bombs exploded at Kenya and Tanzania embassies; seek to show missile attack against him was not hastily improvised because of domestic political calculations; photo (M)

1998-09-06.title: As Ruble Withers, Russians Survive on Barter
1998-09-06.abstract: Russian companies, which have developed special brand of cashless capitalism, are counting more than ever on barter to survive in face of tumbling ruble; barter, ridiculed by Western economists, reflects gritty termination to get by without bank credits and investment capital; increasingly involves schemes in which goods are shipped across nation in complex swaps that can involve dozen or more companies; map; diagram; photo (M)

1998-09-07.title: Living for Rice, Begging for Rice
1998-09-07.abstract: Growing number of Indonesians can no longer afford to buy rice, nation's basic food; price has skyrocketed as poor harvests, devalued currency and badly frayed distribution system combined to create dire shortage; experts are warning that rice is biggest threat to stability as nation struggles to regain political and economic footing; food riots have erupted in eastern Java and could spread unless price of rice falls; map; photo (M)

1998-09-07.title: RUSSIAN NOMINEE WARNS OF CHAOS IF HE IS REJECTED
1998-09-07.abstract: Viktor S Chernomyrdin, facing Parliament vote on his nomination as Prime Minister, warns Russia is in fiscal nose dive and that further political squabbling could lead to social chaos, television interview; says economic situation is so dire that 'no classical anti-crisis' steps apply; apparently defends seemingly contradictory parts of his fiscal plan; he and Acting Deputy Prime Min Boris G Fyodorov detail plan; Fyodorov calls for again making American dollar legal tender in Russia; photos (M)

1998-09-07.title: Progress in Investigation of Embassy Blasts, Officials Say
1998-09-07.abstract: Federal Bureau of Investigation and Tanzanian police say probers have arrested two more suspects in United States Embassy bombing and are making progress in determining how bomb was made and taken to embassy; arrest warrant reportedly has been issued in New York for Abdallah Mohammed Fadhul, Comoros Islands businessman reportedly sought in attack on American Embassy in Nairobi, Kenya; FBI and Comoros police raid two homes, Moroni, searching for him (M)

1998-09-07.title: Phnom Penh Journal; High-Minded Machiavelli, or Cambodia's Spoiler
1998-09-07.abstract: Interview with Sam Rainsy, who has brought Cambodia to standstill with vigorous campaign of protests and rallies and political maneuvers that threaten to block formation of new government; Rainsy, who finished distant third in recent elections, refuses to even consider compromise with rivals; has persuaded Prince Norodom Ranariddh, who placed second in elections, to join him in protests and in refusal to form needed coalition with Prime Min Hun Sen; photo (M)i

1998-09-07.title: I.R.A. Cannot Guarantee Disarmament, Adams Says
1998-09-07.abstract: Sinn Fein leader Gerry Adams says he cannot guarantee Irish Republic Army will agree to disarm during talks in new Northern Ireland Assembly; hard-line stand contrasts with his remarks on eve of Pres Clinton's visit, when he stressed that sectarian warfare is thing of past; Protestant leader David Trimble is unusually positive about outlook for eventual IRA disarmament, BBC interview (M)

1998-09-08.title: Communists Still Seeking True Identity
1998-09-08.abstract: Even though Russia's Communist Party, largest in Parliament, contemptuously rejected for second time Pres Boris Yeltsin's choice of Viktor S Chernomyrdin as Prime Minister--demanding restoration of some of its old power and threatening to block new government--it is not clear to party members what needs to be done to alleviate economic and political crisis; some unreconstructed radicals yearn to oust Yeltsin's administration and restore monopolistic control of Soviet days, others want state control of major industries and banks but would leave part of private sector untouched; some are so house-trained that Yeltsin long ago invited them to join his Cabinet (M)

1998-09-08.title: Richardson Reflects on His U.N. Days
1998-09-08.abstract: Interview with Bill Richardson, outgoing US Ambassador to UN; photo (M)

1998-09-08.title: Central Banker's Resignation Deepens Financial Chasm
1998-09-08.abstract: Article on resignation of Russian central bank chairman, Sergei K Dubinin; resignation leaves large gap at top of Russia's economic hierarchy at time when it can least afford it; his erratic handling of Russia's gyrating financial markets and economic collapse was responsible for his undoing; photo (M)

1998-09-08.title: Congo Peace Talks Finish With Agreement Reported
1998-09-08.abstract: Zimbabwe summit of African leaders, aimed at negotiating peace settlement for Congo, ends on optimistic note; Zambian Pres Frederick Chiluba, chairman of group, announces that all parties are in general agreement, but refuses to give details of pact; just how much was resolved remains unclear; photo of Congo Pres Laurent Kabila (M)

1998-09-08.title: As Tension Rises in Cambodia, Troops Fire Into Crowds
1998-09-08.abstract: Cambodia's post-election standoff erupts into turmoil and gunfire after Prime Min Hun Sen orders arrest of his opponents and after security agents fire into crowds of protesters in capital of Phnom Penh, killing at least one person; Sam Rainsy, organizer of protests and chief target of Hun Sen's threats, takes refuge at UN office in capital; has led increasingly assertive protest against result of July Parliamentary election, which he says was marred by irregularities; has threatened to block formation of new Government by denying Hun Sen chance to put together coalition of two-thirds of Parliamentary seats; photo (M)

1998-09-09.title: Patch of Desert Is Key to Mideast Talks' Next Step
1998-09-09.abstract: Article on arid parcel of land in Judean Desert, so-called nature reserve that is home to bedouin nomads, that is focus of renewed efforts by US and others to revive long-stalled peace process; parcel, southeast of Hebron, is latest three percent of West Bank that Israelis have been loath to surrender and Palestinians hesitant to accept; map; photo (M)

1998-09-09.title: As Ruble Crashes, Hangover Hurts a Lot
1998-09-09.abstract: Article focuses on big toll Russia's financial turmoil taking toll on small but new and visible middle class, especially in Moscow, which arose during city's giddy post-Communist expansion and spent money 'like there was no tomorrow;' photo (M)

1998-09-09.title: Chief U.N. Arms Inspector Disturbed by Criticism of Ex-Inspector
1998-09-09.abstract: Richard Butler, chief UN arms inspector for Iraq, says he is disturbed by US Senate testimony of former American inspector Scott Ritter, who resigned on August 26 alleging that Clinton and Administration and UN were too conciliatory toward Iraq and had undermined inspection system; says Ritter's testimony was often inaccurate in chronology and detail and had damaged commission; says Ritter inflated his role and made misleading statements about Butler's dealings with Security Council officials, and particularly with US Sec of State Madeleine K Albright (M)

1998-09-09.title: Cambodia Security Forces Tear Down Protesters' Tent City
1998-09-09.abstract: Cambodian troops, without warning, tear down tent city, erected two weeks ago in capital of Phnom Penh by protestors contesting results of recent Parliamentary elections; number of people are injured but no deaths reported; destruction appears to break agreement reached hours earlier that would have produced peaceful end to sit-in against Government of Prime Min Hun Sen; photo (M)

1998-09-09.title: Talks on a Cease-Fire in Congo End; Peace Gets Only Lip Service
1998-09-09.abstract: Despite official announcement of cease-fire agreement in Congo, latest peace negotiations in Zimbabwe end much like others in recent weeks, with nothing more than joint statement endorsing peace as ultimate goal; talks succeeded in bringing together all parties in conflict, but several issues apparently left participants deeply divided (S)

1998-09-10.title: Clinton Signs Off on Digital Advance
1998-09-10.abstract: DUBLIN—  When President Bill Clinton applied his digital signature to a joint communiqué with Prime Minister Bertie Ahern of Ireland in Dublin on Friday, the two not only made a little bit of digital history, they also gave an unwitting lift to the cause of strong encryption.

1998-09-10.title: U.N. Keeps Sanctions on Iraq, Citing Its Balking of Monitors
1998-09-10.abstract: Security Council votes unanimously to deprive Iraq of any hope of having sanctions lifted until Pres Saddam Hussein resumes cooperation with United Nations arms inspectors; resolution also calls for complete review of Iraqi relations with United Nations, but not until arms inspectors of UN Special Commission and International Atomic Energy Agency are allowed to resume their work in full (M)

1998-09-10.title: Iran President Will Not Bar Use of Force On Afghans
1998-09-10.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami of Iran warns that his country will act 'whichever way we see fit' to resolve potentially explosive dispute with Taliban, militant Islamic movement that controls most of neighboring Afghanistan; says United States has no business warning others against use of military force; Iran-based diplomats and independent experts in Iran say they continue to regard all-out war as unlikely; but they do not rule out possibility that Iran would carry out limited punitive strike unless Afghanistan answers questions about dozens of Iranians who disappeared during fighting in Afghanistan last month (M)

1998-09-10.title: Rebuking Ex-Arms Inspector, Albright Defends U.S. Role
1998-09-10.abstract: Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright defends Clinton Administration's Iraq policy at American Legion meeting in New Orleans; hits back at William Scott Ritter Jr, former UN arms inspector, and other critics who accused her of trying to scuttle inspections of Iraq's weapons sites; says they are sincere, but they are wrong when they blame America for world's failure to uncover full truth about Iraq's weapons of mass destruction; photo (M)

1998-09-10.title: Beijing Dissident's Wife Is Hauled Away Near U.N. Rights Chief
1998-09-10.abstract: Security agents drag wife of jailed Chinese dissident Liu Nianchun through lobby of Hilton Hotel in Beijing and detain her, moments before visiting United Nations human rights commissioner gives speech in nearby room on democracy and freedom; wife, Chu Hailan, had sought to press case of her husband, labor rights campaigner who has been in prison for more than three years without trial; UN High Commissioner for Human Rights, Mary Robinson, later tells reporters she was deeply concerned when she learned of incident and was assured by Chinese oficial that Chu had been released (M)

1998-09-11.title: Students in Jakarta Continue Protests
1998-09-11.abstract: Hundreds of students, ringed by soldiers, stage noisy but peaceful demonstration across from Presidential Palace, Jakarta, in third day of protests aimed at Pres B J Habibie and his Cabinet (S)

1998-09-11.title: Iran Holds Taliban Responsible for 9 Diplomats' Deaths
1998-09-11.abstract: Iran angrily says it will hold militant Taliban movement responsible for deaths of nine Iranian diplomats whose bodies were recovered by Taliban in northern Afghanistan; Taliban says that Iranians were killed by renegade forces who acted without orders (M)

1998-09-11.title: Volkswagen to Create $12 Million Fund for Nazi-Era Laborers
1998-09-11.abstract: Volkswagen AG, faced with new lawsuit over use of slave labor during World War II, is to announce plans on Sept 11 to set up fund of about $12 million to compensate surviving workers; it will be overseen by Shimon Peres, former Prime of Israel, according to people working with company; move comes at time when American lawyers have filed class-action suits over slave labor against Volkswagen and many other German industrial companies; most of companies acknowledge that they used slave laborers during Hitler's Third Reich, but they contend they were forced to do so by Govt (M)

1998-09-11.title: In a New Sign of Peace in Ulster, Troops Will Halt Belfast Patrols
1998-09-11.abstract: British troops will stop patrolling Belfast area this weekend and some paramilitary prisoners will be released in next few days (S)

1998-09-11.title: Primakov Well Known and Respected by U.S.
1998-09-11.abstract: Clinton Adm welcomes nomination of Foreign Min Yevgeny M Primakov as Russia's next Prime Min, with officials describing him as a sure-footed survivor whose appointment could help defuse a political and economic crisis that had threatened to bring down Pres Boris N Yeltsin (S)

1998-09-12.title: Thugs Attack Demonstrators In Cambodia; One Shot Dead
1998-09-12.abstract: Band of pro-Government thugs beat and shoot at opposition demonstrators in Phnom Penh, Cambodia; military warns it may take stronger action, saying it cannot allow 'worrying anarchy' of continuing protests; photo (M)

1998-09-12.title: Mexico Drug Trafficker Slain; Major Figure Near U.S. Border
1998-09-12.abstract: Rafael Munoz Talavera, Mexican drug trafficker, is slain near Ciudad Juarez, across Border from El Paso, Tex; body is found in parked automobile with gunshot wounds; Mexican police say he fought for control of major drug cartel after he was freed from charges of being master smuggler in largest cocaine seizure in 1989; photo (M)

1998-09-12.title: Yeltsin's Choice Easily Ratified; A Shift to Left
1998-09-12.abstract: Russian Parliament confirms Yevgeny M Primakov as Prime Minister and approves Viktor A Gerashchenko, Soviet-era banker and nemesis of Yeltsin's team of Western-style reformers in early 1990's, as chairman of Russian central bank; endorsement of two of Communist party's hand-picked candidates for top posts signals sharp shift toward more socialist economic model; Primakov says he will put together Government of unity, whose first task is to pull country out of deepening social and economic crisis and restore Moscow's dwindling authority over rest of country; photo (M)

1998-09-12.title: Motozintla Journal; Nature's Fury Overwhelms a Mexican Backwater
1998-09-12.abstract: Many days of intense rain causes flooding in village of Motozintla in Chiapas State, Mexico; 41 people have died and 70 are missing; many residents are edging toward panic as hunger closes in on them and their children; map; photo (M)

1998-09-12.title: Peacemakers in Bosnia Putting Hope in a Serb
1998-09-12.abstract: Diplomats say they need support of Ljubisa Savic, Serbian politician and police chief of Bijeljina, Bosnia, for mission to bring justice and democracy to country; say he is most important man in second-largest Serbian-controlled part of Bosnia; his paramilitary forces helped take over city six years ago, during which almost everyone who was not Serb was terrorized, killed, robbed or expelled; map (M)

1998-09-13.title: For Primakov, Now Comes the Hard Part: Finding a Plan That Will Save Russia
1998-09-13.abstract: Celestine Bohlen analysis of the conflicting economic plans and emergency measures that Yevgeny M Primakov has inherited on taking office as Prime Minister; photo (M)

1998-09-13.title: After a Lift, Cuban Church Has a Letdown
1998-09-13.abstract: Roman Catholic Church in Cuba shows new vitality in wake of Pope John Paul II's visit, but Pres Fidel Castro has not shown much new flexibility since then in response to church requests for greater freedom; communications between the church and government have improved; ongoing organizational efforts by Catholics underscore the challenge that the church potentially poses to Communist Party; neither Saneria worshipers nor Cuba's Protestant sects have developed any natinal structures to speak of; photos (M)

1998-09-13.title: Iran Angered By a Promise That Proved To Be Hollow
1998-09-13.abstract: Iran is angered at killing of diplomats posted to consulate in Mazar-I-Sharif, northern Afghan town that was headquarters of an Iranian-backed militia alliance; Taliban, Afghanistan's militant Sunni Islamic movement, gave Iran a pledge that diplomats would be safe, but it now admits that 9 and perhaps all 11 of them were killed by Afghan soldiers that captured town; Taliban forces reportedly reach northern outskirts of Bamiyan, last Afghan city held by Iranian-backed opposition and home to thousands of Shiite Muslims with whom Iran feels a religious bond (M)

1998-09-13.title: Cambodian Prince Offers Way to End Stalemate
1998-09-13.abstract: Investors are pouring money into Mozambique, encouraged by the strict economic management practiced by Government and its success so far in privatizing the economy; country is on brink of a boom, although is remains one of the world's poorest countries; its role in world economy is still much like its colonial one: being exploited by foreign buyers of raw materials; maps and graphs (M)

1998-09-13.title: In Tourism Transit Disputes, Grenada Is the Little Guy
1998-09-13.abstract: Airlines and cruise ships plying the Caribbean exercise their leverage over its tourist-dependent islands with mixed results; American Airlines is able to exact a $1.5 million subsidy from Government of Grenada, using threat of suspending daily flights; however, cruise lines may not be able to play one island against the other in order to avoid a $1.50-per-passenger charge being demanded to help pay to clean up cruise ship waste; islands announced the fee simultaneously; map (M)

1998-09-14.title: Conservatives Win Decisive Victory in Bavarian Election
1998-09-14.abstract: German Chancellor Helmut Kohl's prospects for re-election receives significant lift as Bavaria's conservative Christian Social Union wins decisive victory over Social Democratic Party in state-wide elections in Bavaria; exit polls suggest Social Union received more than 52 percent of vote, compared with 29.5 percent for opposition Social Democrats (M)

1998-09-14.title: Two Sides of Cambodia March and Move to Bargaining Table
1998-09-14.abstract: Week of chaos and violence in Cambodia's capital of Phnom Penh that has paralyzed city takes on ritual flavor today as post-election maneuvers moved toward bargaining table (M)

1998-09-14.title: After 29 Die in Bombing, Old Haven Of I.R.A. Turns Its Back on Bloodshed
1998-09-14.abstract: Dundalk, Ireland, former haven of Irish Republican Army, has turned its back on violence following August 15 bombing in Omagh that killed 29 people in most deadly attack in province's three decades of conflict; town's defiant rejection of its past association with bloodshed is being cited as emblem of what is happening throughout all of Ireland; map; photo (M)

1998-09-14.title: Much of Chiapas Coast Isolated After Floods
1998-09-14.abstract: Coastline of southern Mexican state of Chiapas remains isolated after week of floods caused by heaviest rainfall in decades (S)

1998-09-14.title: Mexico City Journal; Anniversary of '68 Massacre Brings Facts to Light
1998-09-14.abstract: Official version of massacre of demonstrators by security forces in Mexico City in October 1968, which seared conscience of entire generation of Mexicans, comes under attack as never before in new book by academic, Sergio Aguayo Quezada, who blames former Pres Gustavo Diaz Ordaz for orchestrating violence and blaming victims to justify crackdown on budding democracy movement; Government has resisted every attempt to investigate horror, insisting that students provoked bloodshed by attacking security forces; photo (M)

1998-09-15.title: Holbrooke Discloses Gift of Free Lodging
1998-09-15.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, nominee for chief American delegate to United Nations, amends his financial disclosure statements to reflect gift worth more than $12,000 in lodgings from former American Ambassador to Switzerland, M Larry Lawrence; Clinton Administration officials say initial failure to list gift poses no threat to nomination; Pres Clinton reiterates support for nomination (M)

1998-09-15.title: Iraq Assembly Threatens End To All Inspection Cooperation
1998-09-15.abstract: Iraqi Parliament threatens to stop all remaining cooperation with United Nations over weapons inspections; demands that Security Council reverse recent decision to stop all reviews of sanctions until Iraq allows on-site inspections to resume; photo (M)

1998-09-15.title: Clinton's Speech on World Economies: '8 Months Later the Clouds Are Closer'
1998-09-15.abstract: Excerpts from Pres Clinton's speech before Council on Foreign Relations, which he warned about threat to US and world economy from economic turmoil on three continents (M)

1998-09-15.title: Indonesian Demonstrators Oppose Military's Role
1998-09-15.abstract: Nearly 200 students stage sit-in in one of busiest thoroughfares in Jakarta, Indonesia, in move to pressure Government to take important powers away from military; call for end to armed forces' role in nation's political affairs (S)

1998-09-15.title: U.S. Calls North Korean Rocket a Failed Satellite
1998-09-15.abstract: Clinton Administration and US defense officials now believe North Korea tried but failed to launch small satellite into orbit when it fired multistage rocket in an arc over Japan on Aug 31; defense officials say rocket was significantly more powerful than first believed, having three stages instead of two, and that final stage used solid fuel--significant technological leap for North Korea (S)

1998-09-16.title: German Socialists Turn From Slogans to Jingles
1998-09-16.abstract: Gerhard Schroder and his Social Democratic Party are running one of slickest and most American-style political advertising campaigns ever in Germany; media drive combines cozy images of happy Germany and edgy criticism of Chancellor Helmut Kohl; marks major departure from dull and didactic fare typical of German campaigns; contrasts with Kohl's old-school ads; photos (M)

1998-09-16.title: U.N. Rights Official, Back From China, Sees Better Attitude
1998-09-16.abstract: United Nations Human Rights Comr Mary Robinson, completing first visit to China, says that despite continuing abuses, Chinese leaders show new willingess to discuss nation's rights record, news conference, Beijing; lauds China's willingness to let commission work in nation; rights groups react with cautious optimism (M)

1998-09-16.title: Elite Mexican Drug Officers Said to Be Tied to Traffickers
1998-09-16.abstract: American officials say ambitious effort to overhaul Mexico's corrupt law-enforcement system has been thrown into turmoil by disclosure that top investigators of elite American-trained police unit may have ties to drug traffickers; lie-detector tests administered by American experts at Mexican Government request have implicated most of unit's senior officials, raising concern that data shared with unit may have been compromised; photo (M)

1998-09-16.title: China Military Chief Gives Pledges to U.S.
1998-09-16.abstract: Chinese Gen Zhang Wannianmeets with Defense Sec William S Cohen, Washington, and pledges China will move ahead with modest joint military exercises and exchanges agreed to at Pres Clinton's summit meeting with Pres Jiang Zemin; he and Cohen sign pact to share data on tackling environmental problems caused by armed forces; meets with national security adviser Samuel R Berger, meeting briefly attended by Clinton (S)

1998-09-16.title: Kosovo's Crisis Is Bad, and Getting Worse
1998-09-16.abstract: Clinton Administration officials says United States must decide quickly on whether to use military force to end Serbian assault that has forced mass exodus of ethnic Albanians from homes in Kosovo, Serbia, to bar thousands of starvation deaths this winter; charity groups say situation grows more desparate, with Serbs nearly continuously shelling ethnic Albanian villages; Gen John P Jumper says NATO air strikes would by easy to execute, but might not be effective (M)

1998-09-17.title: U.S. and Iran, in Further Thaw, Will Join in U.N. Afghan Talks
1998-09-17.abstract: United States and Iran could have their highest-level diplomatic contact since 1979, with Sec Madeleine K Albright and Min Kamal Kharazi expected to attend United Nations meeting on crisis on Afghanistan's border with Iraq; Clinton Administration says meeting will not result in direct conversations between Albright and Kharazi; Sec Gen Kofi Annan organizes meeting, also inviting foreign ministers of Afghanistan's other neighbors (M)

1998-09-17.title: Tequisquiapan Journal; Across the Land, Down the Years, 'Viva Mexico!'
1998-09-17.abstract: Mexico marks Independence Day, its most important patriotic holiday; Mayor Jose Antonio Mejia, who at 27 may be Mexico's youngest mayor, leads festivites in Tequisquiapan; map; photo (M)

1998-09-17.title: Serbs in Bosnia Appear to Shun Favorite of U.S.
1998-09-17.abstract: Preliminary returns indicate Bosnia Serb voters have rejected Pres Biljana Plavsic, key player in American strategy in Bosnia, in favor of Nikola Poplasen, extreme nationalist, in repudiation of Western goals of integrating Serbs, Croats and Muslims; indicate moderates may hold their slight majority in Bosnian Serb legislature; major races in Muslim-Croat Federation are too close to call, but Muslim leader, Alija Izetbegovic, will clearly continue on federation's three-member presidency (M)

1998-09-17.title: Relief Falls Short After Storms Ravage Mexican Villages
1998-09-17.abstract: Many flood-ravished communities along 160-mile Chiapas, Mexico, coast remain cut off from overland aid deliveries; Government's helicopter airlift is slowed by new rain and lack of supplies; hungry people fight for aid supplies in some villages; officials say they may never know how many people died in disaster, in which 15 rivers overflowed; photo (M)

1998-09-17.title: Singapore's Senior Minister Angers Malaysians With First of 2 Volumes : Lee Kuan Yew's Controversial Memoirs
1998-09-17.abstract: SINGAPORE—  Lee Kuan Yew, Singapore's senior minister, marked his 75th birthday on Wednesday with the launch of memoirs that have caused fresh tension in relations with Malaysia even before they appeared on sale in bookstores.

1998-09-18.title: China Detains Two Who Applied for New Party
1998-09-18.abstract: Chinese police in Beijing detain two dissidents preparing to apply to register new political party; Ren Wanding has spent more than ten years in custody and Ma Shaohua was student leader at Tiananmen Square protests (S)

1998-09-18.title: Two Kurd Groups Unite Against Baghdad in Pact Brokered by U.S.
1998-09-18.abstract: State Department announces brokering of settlement between two feuding factions of Iraqi Kurds, uniting them against Saddam Hussein; Sec Albright announces accord after meetings in Washington between Massoud Barzani and Jalal Talibani (S)

1998-09-18.title: Burmese Opposition Issues Risky Challenge to the Junta
1998-09-18.abstract: Small group of Burmese opposition politicians say they will act as country's legitimate parliament, and declare null and void all of ruling junta's laws and proclamations; risky challenge to military comes from ten politicians, led by Daw Aung San Suu Kyi, whose party won 1990 elections for parliament that was never allowed to take office; Government does not respond immediately, but tone of recent statements has grown more threatening, describing pro-democracy figures as traitors and terrorists (S)

1998-09-18.title: In Zambia, the Abandoned Generation
1998-09-18.abstract: Article in series Dead Zones describes plight of Zambian children orphaned by AIDS and often cast out by relatives to fend for themselves; in 1997 alone, AIDS orphaned 1.7 million children, more than 90 percent of them in sub-Saharan Africa; photos; demographic map; UN says that Zambia, which has never embraced birth control, has highest proportion of orphans in world, estimated 23 percet of all children under 15; Lukasa has more than 90,000 children on streets, and even those with relatives are often the last to get food, shoes and schooling, and suffer beatings and sexual abuse; harsh lives of several abandoned children described (L)

1998-09-18.title: New Tests Dispute U.S. Finding of Iraq Nerve Gas
1998-09-18.abstract: Swiss and French tests for poison gas on Iraqi missile warheads appear to have reached results contrary to those from American tests that showed presence of VX, deadly nerve agent; Iraq has admitted loading Sarin on weapons, but not VX, and may argue that US tests were rigged (S)

1998-09-19.title: Russia to Pump Huge Ruble Reserve Into Banks
1998-09-19.abstract: Russia's central bank, seeking to restart stalled financial activity, will let insolvent banks use about $1.65 billion in ruble reserves; is gambling that errant banks will use reserves to settle delinquent bills, not buy dollars, move that would further drive down value of ruble; plans to print more rubles to replenish reserve; photo (M)

1998-09-19.title: No Holiday From Fear, Or Hope, In Israel
1998-09-19.abstract: Israelis preparing for Jewish New Year crowd Jerusalem market that was target of many past terrorist bombings, ignoring threat of reprisals for Israeli Army slaying of two Islamic militants; skepticism about outlook for peace softens somewhat on reports that State Sec Madeleine K Albright has promised to meet in United States with Israeli and Palestinian leaders (M)

1998-09-19.title: Caste Hate, and Murder, Outlast Indian Reforms
1998-09-19.abstract: Murder by gang from untouchable subcaste that has gained from 'reservations' of jobs, schooling and political offices for untouchables of member of lower subcaste, Meerut, India, calls attention to ways in which efforts to erase lines of prejudice have made caste more important than ever in India; tensions between subcastes over division of benefits exploded over unsanctioned romance between boy from lower subcaste and girl from more powerful group; map; photos (M)

1998-09-19.title: U.S. Dismayed at Apparent Victory of Hard-Line Bosnian Serb
1998-09-19.abstract: Clinton Administration officials are dismayed by projections that Nikola Poplasen, extreme nationalist, has defeated American-backed incumbent, Biljana Plavsic, in presidential election in Serbian-populated half of Bosnia; his victory could undo American efforts to reintegrate Bosnian Serbs, Croats and Muslims into single nation (M)

1998-09-19.title: Clinton Likely To Delay Trip To Pakistan And India
1998-09-19.abstract: Officials say Pres Clinton is likely to delay trip to India and Pakistan, planned for mid-Nov, because both nations tested nuclear devices in May and have shown little interest in defusing their arms race (M)

1998-09-20.title: After Dispute In Pakistan, Leader Leaves For U.S. Visit
1998-09-20.abstract: Pakistani Prime Min Nawaz Sharif leaves for visit to United States after facing Parliamentary opposition to his proposal to make Islamic justice supreme law of the land (M)

1998-09-20.title: A Humbled Yeltsin Is Fading Away
1998-09-20.abstract: Russian Pres Boris N Yeltsin's clout seems to be ebbing and new Prime Min Yevgeny M Primakov is clearly calling the shots; Yeltsin has become less visible presence, but makes rare appearance in public to attend gala concert at Moscow's Conservatory; he leaves before intermission; photo (M)

1998-09-20.title: Brazilians Fret as Economic Threat Moves Closer
1998-09-20.abstract: Until recently, it seemed that Augusta Aparecida Pettinari's fortunes and the country's economic reform plan ran on parallel tracks, like twin locomotives taking the young receptionist and the modern currency toward a steadily more promising future.    As once-rampant inflation was brought down to negligible levels, Ms. Pettinari joined an emerging class of consumers, whose salaries for the first time maintained their purchasing power from one paycheck to the next. She smiles now as she remembers buying appliances, of going to the movies and of writing a check for a new dress.

1998-09-20.title: OIL RICH, OIL POOR: A special report.; High Claims in Spill Betray Depth of Nigerian Poverty
1998-09-20.abstract: Special report on Mobil oil spill off Nigerian coast in January, which has become subject of vehement dispute and catalyst for pent-up claims fed by poverty and state neglect; as Nigeria approaches delicate transition from military rule to democracy, delta region's long-festering problems pose threat of widespread violence; sabotage against pipelines is becoming routine, and poverty-stricken villagers are making huge financial demands on Mobil; since oil was discovered in delta region 42 years ago, enormous wealth has been taken by Western companies and by country's military rulers; now Mobil is facing billions of dollars in claims from population that feels it has been long ignored and Government of Gen Abdulsalam Abubakar is making conciliatory gestures but initiating little real reform; maps; photos (M)

1998-09-20.title: Swiss Hills Are Alive With Sound of Diesels
1998-09-20.abstract: Switzerland, which because of its location is subject to much of Europe's north-south traffic, is trying to create plan that would balance transport needs of European Union with protecting its scenic valleys from noise and air pollution created by truck traffic (M)

1998-09-21.title: Darkest Hour at U.N. For Richest Deadbeat
1998-09-21.abstract: Pres Clinton, during September 21 visit to UN, will face organization likely to strip US of General Assembly vote by end of 1998 for nonpayment of dues totaling more than $1.5 billion; organization's largest contributor is currently Japan, which kept dues payments up to date despite recession; organization has been kept afloat by delaying payment for peacekeeping forces to Japanese, Europeans and some developing countries and applying funds to operating budget; Pres Clinton is viewed by many UN diplomats and officials as too weak or unwilling to battle hostile Congress to preserve pivotal American role; US remains without chief representative to organization since departure of Bill Richardson and blocking of appointment of Richard C Holbrooke as successor by Republicans (M)

1998-09-21.title: As Investigation Begins, Indonesian Laws Favor Ex-President : Suharto's Wealth May All Be Legal
1998-09-21.abstract: SINGAPORE—  As official Indonesian investigators prepare to question former President Suharto for the first time this week about his wealth, experts are cautioning that any criminal wrongdoing will be hard to prove and that the tracing and recovery of money and assets will be equally difficult, especially if they are outside Indonesia.

1998-09-21.title: Swedish Party Pledging Expanded Welfare Gains Slim Victory
1998-09-21.abstract: Sweden's governing Social Democrats ride pledge to expand country's huge welfare programs to thin victory in national election, signaling shift to left; received 36.5 percent of vote, and conservative Moderates 22.7 percent; though result represents significant drop in support for Social Democrats, who received slightly more than 45 percent of vote in 1994, it appears sufficient to allow them to form alliance with parties farther left; would keep Prime Min Goran Persson in power and frustrate bid by Carl Bildt, Moderate leader, to regain office he occupied from 1991 to 1994 (M)

1998-09-21.title: Demonstration Was Largest in the Capital's History : Malaysia Police Seize Anwar After Anti-Mahathir Protest
1998-09-21.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  Malaysian policemen wearing black ski masks and carrying submachine guns smashed their way into the house of Anwar Ibrahim, the country's former deputy prime minister, late Sunday night and arrested him, just hours after he had led the largest political demonstration in this city's history.

1998-09-21.title: Japan Sees Itself as a Scapegoat Of Washington in the Asia Crisis
1998-09-21.abstract: Japanese officials express irritation at what they perceive as American arrogance and scapegoating of Japan, accusing US of rejecting Japan's overtures to grapple with Asian economic crisis in early stages; bridle at suggestion that they are part of problem rather than solution, and that Japan is culprit in Asian crisis; article notes Japan proposed setting up $100 billion fund to help Southeast Asian countries pull through when crisis was in infancy year ago, but was swiftly rebuffed by US; notes Japan was first major country to pay attention to crisis unfolding in Thailand in summer 1997, and tried without success to get world leaders attending Denver summit to discuss economic instability in Asia; simmering resentments in Tokyo may grow with September 22 summit between Prime Min Keizo Obuchi and Pres Clinton in New York, since one of its purposes is to give Clinton chance to impress on Obuchi importance of rapid action to revive Japan's economy (M)

1998-09-22.title: Iranian President Paints a Picture of Peace and Moderation
1998-09-22.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami of Iran addresses opening session of United Nations General Assembly for first time, portraying Iran as freedom-loving and law-abiding country; asks for help in resolving crisis in neighboring Afghanistan; refrains from criticizing US; his criticisms of Israel are milder than usual; photo (M)

1998-09-22.title: Russians Vow To End Crisis, But Key Posts Go Unfilled
1998-09-22.abstract: Russia's leaders offer more assurances that country's fiscal crisis can be tamed without provoking high inflation or social unrest, but with key positions in new Government unfilled, few people accord promise much weight; neither Pres Boris Yeltsin nor Prime Min Yevgeny Primakov has settled on choice for Finance Minister, perhaps most important post next to Primakov's (M)

1998-09-22.title: Tanzania Charges Two in Bombing of American Embassy
1998-09-22.abstract: Tanzania charges two men with 11 counts of murder in connection with bombing of US Embassy in Dar es Salaam on August 7; they are Mustafa Mahmoud Said Ahmed, Egyptian national, and Rashid Saleh Hemed, Tanzanian; action contrasts with decision by Kenya to extradite two suspects to US; sets up potential diplomatic conflict with US and might hamper investigation (M)

1998-09-22.title: Malaysian Police Break Up Protests on Arrest
1998-09-22.abstract: Malaysian riot police officers, acting to quell biggest show of dissent faced by Prime Min Mahathir Mohamad in his 17 years in office, fire tear gas and water cannons to disperse thousands of people protesting arrest of former Deputy Prime Minister, Anwar Ibrahim; former deputy is being held under Internal Security Act, which allows indefinite imprisonment without charges or trial of people deemed threat to national security; photo (M)

1998-09-22.title: Remodel World Bank And I.M.F., Blair Urges
1998-09-22.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain addresses opening session of UN General Assembly, where he urges revamping of way world's financial institutions deal with global economic turmoil and says procedures of World Bank and International Monetary Fund must be updated (M)

1998-09-23.title: Two Political Leaders Ask I.R.A. to Disarm
1998-09-23.abstract: Irish Prime Min Bertie Ahern and David Trimble, First Minister of new Northern Ireland Assembly, call on Irish Republican Army to agree to begin phased disarmament; Trimble earlier discussed problem with Sinn Fein president Gerry Adams (S)

1998-09-23.title: 'New' Germans Find Escape From Tainted Past
1998-09-23.abstract: Germany's baby boomers have come of age, new elite drawn largely from former West Germany; have grappled with nation's history, understand its shame and see future with self-confidence their parents lack; will define agenda in business, style, arts and, increasingly, in politics as dominant Germany faces new era; photos (M)

1998-09-23.title: U.S. Aide Drives to Valley of Kosovo Refugees
1998-09-23.abstract: Amb Christopher R Hill, Pres Clinton's special envoy for Kosovo, tours valley filled with about 20,000 ethnic Albanian refugees to refute Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic's stand that there is no human crisis in province; says refugees, fearing hostile Serbian forces posted in their villages, have been sleeping in fields, many without shelter; map; photo (M)

1998-09-23.title: Moderate Serbs in Bosnia Remain a Political Force
1998-09-23.abstract: Many foreign diplomats are now saying results of election in Bosnia are not disaster for Western strategy they first thought; others say problem is bad, but may be handled; less hard-line politicians across nation are doing well, despite victory of nationalist Nikola Poplasen in race for Bosnian Serb presidency (M)

1998-09-23.title: Yeltsin Creates Inner Cabinet Of Primakov and 6 Deputies
1998-09-23.abstract: Pres Boris N Yeltsin gives shape to Russia's new Cabinet with decree that creates inner cabinet, or presidium, made up of Prime Min Yevgeny M Primakov and his six top deputies; decree also offers greater voice to regional leaders; elevates to Cabinet status Central Bank's new head, Viktor Gerashchenko, and Academy of Sciences head Yuri Osipov; Primakov, having trouble assembling Government, names Valentina Matviyenko Deputy Prime Minister for social issues, post rejected by at least three leading politicians; she will be first woman in top Government post; photo (M)

1998-09-24.title: Owner and Union Can't Agree on Saving Carrier : 'Everything Is Over,' as Philippine Airlines Shuts Down
1998-09-24.abstract: Philippine Airlines, the oldest carrier in Asia, went out of business at midnight Wednesday after 11th-hour talks mediated by the government failed to resolve differences between the Philippines' richest man and its toughest labor union.

1998-09-24.title: A Centrist Shifts to Left To Attract German Votes
1998-09-24.abstract: German election remains too close to call, with polls suggesting weariness with Chancellor Helmut Kohl's long rule is partly offset with doubts about challenger Gerhard Schroder; Schroder, centrist, shifts oratory to left; photo (M)

1998-09-24.title: Defying Warnings, Serbs Press Attacks on Kosovo Albanians
1998-09-24.abstract: Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic, scoffing at warnings that West might use force to end fighting in Kosovo, presses ahead with tank and artillery offensive; chases some 10,000 ethnic Albanian civilians into hills and sets fire to many of their homes; new refugees spend night in cold and damp with no shelter and little food; map; photo (M)

1998-09-24.title: Swing Vote in East Worries Germans
1998-09-24.abstract: Former East German Communists could win enough votes in German national elections to play important swing role and have good chance of becoming minority partner in governing coalition in Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania; could make it hard for Gerhard Schroder to form majority governing coalition by stealing votes from his left-of-center Social Democrats; Schroder has ruled out coalition with ex-Communists, move that would be political suicide in western Germany; map (M)

1998-09-24.title: San Giovanni Rotondo Journal; Saint or No, an Old-Time Monk Mesmerizes Italy
1998-09-24.abstract: Padre Pio, Capuchin monk and mystic who died in 1968, attracts over six million pilgrims a year to remote mountaintop monastery in southern Italy and his hometown, San Giovanni Rotondo; Pio, not yet recognized as saint by Vatican, has vast, worldwide following that blends medieval piety with kind of modern cult celebrity and commercialism; some 70,000 people gather in San Giovanni for vigil on anniversary of his death; map; photos (M)

1998-09-25.title: Iran Drops Rushdie Death Threat, And Britain Renews Teheran Ties
1998-09-25.abstract: Iran drops death threat imposed on British author Salman Rushdie in 1989 by Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini, and Britain responds by restoring full diplomatic relations; Foreign Min Kamal Kharrazi asserts that Govt 'has no intention, nor is it going to take any action whatsoever, to threaten the life of the author of The Satanic Verses or anybody associated with his work, not will it encourage or assist anybody to do so'; although an Iranian foundation continues to offer $2.5 million bounty for death of 51-year-old Indian-born British author, whose novel Kharrazi says still offends Iranians, Kharrazi says his Govt dissociates itself from any reward that has been offered; Rushdie, interviewed in London, says 'It's over' and 'The fatwa will be left to wither on the vine' (M)

1998-09-25.title: Russia Warns It Might Default if Western Aid Is Withheld
1998-09-25.abstract: Russian Govt, as it lays out ways to spend its way out of roiling economic crisis, takes harsh line with international creditors, warning them of potential default on its long-term debt if more Western aid is not delivered (M)

1998-09-25.title: Peggy's Cove Journal; On Nova Scotia's Shore, Will Sadness Be Forever?
1998-09-25.abstract: Scene in Peggy's Cove, Nova Scotia, described; residents of Peggy's Cove and other villages along coast will always remember those anxious, terrifying and deeply saddening first days after crash of Swissair Flight 111 on Sept 2; but hordes of reporters and satellite trucks are gone, tourist buses have come back, fishermen have gone back to their boats; everyone wants to know whether Peggy's Cove will ever be the same again; photo of tourists looking out at crash site; map (M)

1998-09-25.title: I.M.F. Resists 'Blackmail' by Russia and U.S.
1998-09-25.abstract: Senior official of IMF warns that he will neither submit to 'blackmail' of Russian leaders who threaten national default if they are denied billions of dollars in aid, nor let Congress 'impose conditions' on fund in return for $18 billion in new contributions from United States (M)

1998-09-25.title: Meciar Turns Out the Stars to Get Votes
1998-09-25.abstract: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia—  As Prime Minister Vladimir Meciar of Slovakia cut the ribbon on a short stretch of highway near here last week, the German supermodel Claudia Schiffer flashed her million-dollar smile and said Slovakia was a beautiful country.

1998-09-26.title: Santa Catarina Journal; Drug Gangs' Deadly Toll: Indian Villagers in Baja
1998-09-26.abstract: Article on drug violence in Santa Catarina and neighboring towns in Baja California, Mexico, notes that 18 members of two Mexican Indian families were recently killed in drug-related massacre in El Sauzal; map; photo (M)

1998-09-26.title: Author Banned By British Air
1998-09-26.abstract: British Airways says it will not permit writer Salman Rushdie to fly on its planes, despite Iran's assurances that it will not carry out its death sentence against him (S)

1998-09-26.title: A Campaign Secret:The Emergence of Right-Wing Extremism
1998-09-26.abstract: DRESDEN—  Throughout their campaigns, Helmut Kohl and Gerhard Schroeder kept clear of the issue of emerging right-wing extremism with an agility they usually reserved for avoiding specifics on how they would create new jobs, save money and sustain Germany's enormous social welfare system all at the same time.

1998-09-26.title: Vague, Yes, But Schroder Could Unseat Chancellor
1998-09-26.abstract: Gerhard Schroder, with promise to restore 'social justice to Germany,' winds up his campaign to replace German Chancellor Helmut Kohl in elections on Sept 27; despite erosion of his lead, Schroder appears to have better chance of success than any of his four predecessors as Social Democratic candidates who tried to defeat Kohl over last 16 years (M)

1998-09-26.title: Yeltsin Plugs a Hole in His Kremlin Team, but Then Two New Ones Appear
1998-09-26.abstract: Pres Boris N Yeltsin names Mikhail Zadornov as Finance Minister; he held same job under last Prime Minister, Sergi Kiriyenko; Deputy Prime Min Aleksandr Shokhin resigns in response to appointment after mere nine days on job; Dmitri Yasilyev, head of Russian securities regulation commission, also resigns; Shokhin photo (M)

1998-09-27.title: How Did Pretoria Err? Lesotho Counts the Ways
1998-09-27.abstract: Analysis of the missteps by South Africa that caused its military intervention in Lesotho to go terribly wrong; intervention was intended to quell Lesotho's internal political struggles but has instead created large-scale economic and diplomatic problems that are going to cost South Africa millions and take years to repair; photo; map (M)

1998-09-27.title: Public Face of Terror Suspect: Low-Key Family Man
1998-09-27.abstract: Wahid el Hage, the man accused of being a leader of Osama bin Landen's terrorist organization in Kenya, and Haroun Fazil, who is accused of being one of his top aides, have been leading lives of middle-class family men, not young, unemployed firebrands usually associated with terrorism; el Hage is linked indirectly to mysterious 1991 murder of Mustapha Shalabi in Brooklyn; Shalabi had been Sheik Abdel-Rahman's sponsor when he entered US, but they fell out when Sheik demanded that part of the money they were raising for Islamic rebels fighting Russians in Afghanistan be diverted to effort to overthrow Pres Hosni Mubarak of Egypt (M)

1998-09-27.title: Macao Astir Over Troops In Handover To Beijing
1998-09-27.abstract: Colonial leaders of Macao contend that China's announcement that it will deploy troops in Macao after it takes possession of colony on Dec 20, 1999, represents a new interpretation of 1987 joint declaration that set terms for handover; fear troops will be used more for law enforcement than defense; most residents of Macao, however, are exhausted by colony's gang wars and might actually welcome troops (M)

1998-09-27.title: In Kosovo Death Chronicles, Serb Tactic Revealed
1998-09-27.abstract: Human rights officials chronicle what they say is arbitrary arrests and torture of ethnic Albanian civilians by Serb authorities seeking to discourage popular support of a separatist guerrilla force fighting for independence in Kosovo province; say extrajudicial methods used by officials under control of Yugoslav President, Slobodan Milosevic, run parallel to military intimidation by special police force and Yugoslav Army; photos; map (M)

1998-09-27.title: Afghanistan's Opium Output Drops Sharply, U.N. Survey Shows
1998-09-27.abstract: Pino Alarcchi, executive director of United Nations Drug Control Program, reports that bad weather and tensions with Iran have slashed opium production in Afghanistan and hampered its opium exports for European heroin makets; adds, however, that poppy cultivation is up and has spread to two eastern provinces where it had not been report earlier; says Taliban's religious leaders want to cooperate with UN but movement is too fragmented to act (M)

1998-09-28.title: Europeans Congratulate Both Winner And Loser
1998-09-28.abstract: European leaders congratulate both Gerhard Schroeder and Chancellor Helmut Kohl, who Schroeder defeated in German national elections; there is widespread sense that move by German voters to left after generation is historic turning point that is not so much about ideology as about German self-confidence and assertiveness (M)

1998-09-28.title: Calculated Risk: U.S. and I.M.F. Lead Push for Brazil Bailout Plan
1998-09-28.abstract: Clinton Administration and International Monetary Fund are arranging package of more than $30 billion in loans for Brazil to limit damage to Latin America's biggest economy from Asian financial crisis, even though bailout plan is calculated risk that could sink country into deep recession; package is aimed at preserving value of Brazilian currency and reversing withdrawals by increasingly nervous foreign lenders; Brazil has become front line in struggle to halt spreading financial crisis, because of its power to pull down markets and plunge countries into recession; stakes for US are considerable since its economy dominates Latin America, which accounts for 20 percent of America's exports, and is home to thousands of American-owned factories; looming opposition to financing plan, both in Republican-controlled Congress and among private lenders, discussed (M)

1998-09-28.title: GERMAN VOTERS END AN ERA, REJECT KOHL AFTER 16 YEARS AND PICK A SOCIAL DEMOCRAT
1998-09-28.abstract: Gerhard Schroeder defeats incumbent Helmut Kohl to become Chancellor, ending era in Germany and opening new one in Europe, where center-left governments currently dominate continent's politics; decisive victory after hard-fought campaign is seen as clear expression of weariness of Germans with Kohl's 16-year rule; reflects new priorities that confront continent grappling with competitive pressures of global economy, overburdened welfare systems and high unemployment; Schroeder's Social Democrats gained estimated 40.9 percent of vote, up from 34.6 percent in 1994, and Christian Democrats 35.1 percent, or six-percent decline; Schroeder, whose campaign stressed joblessness and state of economy, faces central issue of how to adjust Germany's expensive welfare system to global competition without dismantling it and at same time create jobs; photo; chart (M)

1998-09-28.title: Albright Meets With Netanyahu And Arafat for Joint Talks
1998-09-28.abstract: US Sec of State Madeleine K Albright meets jointly with Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat in New York to push for agreement on key details of long-stalled peace agreement between Israel and Palestinians; photo (M)

1998-09-28.title: A Top Lender in Japan Files for Bankruptcy
1998-09-28.abstract: Japan Leasing Corp, Japan's second-largest leasing company and affiliate of troubled Long-Term Credit Bank of Japan Ltd, files for bankruptcy, becoming country's biggest postwar bankruptcy (M)

1998-09-29.title: New German Leader to Seek Coalition With the Greens
1998-09-29.abstract: Germany's Chancellor-elect Gerhard Schroeder will seek to form coalition Government with environmentalist Green Party, opening way for first 'Red-Green' Government in 49-year history of postwar republic; final election results show Schroeder's Social Democrats and Greens sweeping to comfortable 21-seat majority in 656-member Parliament as weariness with Helmut Kohl's 16-year rule and anger over high unemployment prompted decisive vore for change; photo (M)

1998-09-29.title: A Top Iranian Aide Rejects U.S. Overture on New Ties
1998-09-29.abstract: Iran's Foreign Min, Kamal Kharrazi, in speech before Asia Society in New York City, rebuffs overture by US to re-establish relations between both countries; accuses Clinton Administration of clinging to outdated policy of hostility toward Iran; notes new tone toward Iran by Pres Clinton and Sec of State Madeleine K Albright, but says language alone cannot provide basis for invitation to political dialogue (M)

1998-09-29.title: The Right-Leftist on Top: Gerhard Schroder
1998-09-29.abstract: Man in the News biographical profile of Germany's Chancellor-elect Gerhard Schroeder, whose biggest challenge will be to define who he really is; begun political career as Marxist in late 1960's, but evolved into immaculately tailored politician who uses television to project success as much as social justice; prides himself on having modernized left-of-center Social Democratic Party; was born in Lower Saxony in 1944; grew up poor; never knew father who died fighting for Germany in Romania; worked as hardware salesman before finishing secondary school at 22; put himself through law school at Gottingen University with support from Government; earned law degree in 1976, when he was 32; photo (M)

1998-09-29.title: Germany's Shift East
1998-09-29.abstract: News analysis says victory by Social Democrat Gerhard Schroeder in German national elections to become Chancellor-elect ushers in new phase in German political life; victory represents eastward shift away from conservative Rhineland culture of Chancellor Helmut Kohl and first postwar Chancellor, Konrad Adenauer, toward as yet untried forms of government (M)

1998-09-29.title: Jerusalem Journal; Aged Rabbi at Center of an Unholy War of Words
1998-09-29.abstract: Rabbi Yitzhak Kadouri is playing bizarrely important role in Israel's political life; Iraqi-born mystic who is over 100 years old, has been used at festive political rallies to win votes for Shas, religious party that represents Sephardic Jews and has grown to be third largest political entity in Israel; wears fixed smile and dreamy gaze, which some take to be faith and others to be senility, and has recently been caught in unholy war of words among Israel's leaders over boundaries between religion and politics, rationalism and mysticism; many leaders have questioned Kadouri's lucidity and have complained about his exploitation; photo (M)

1998-09-30.title: Taliban's Man in Queens Wants the U.N. to Listen
1998-09-30.abstract: Taliban diplomat Abdul Hakeem Mujahid says United Nations is using seat of Afghanistan as tool of pressure on Islamic emirate to change its policies and to impose on it kind of coalition government what will be consequently secular government; says defeat of remaining forces of US-backed Mujahedeen armies is movement's first priority (M)

1998-09-30.title: For Pakistan And India, Atom Pact Is Hard Sell
1998-09-30.abstract: Celia W Dugger analysis of willingness expressed by Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee of India and Prime Min Nawaz Sharif of Pakistan to sign nuclear test-ban treaty; says the two leaders will need to convince their restive publics that they got something substantial in return; notes that response to their speeches at United Nations, in which they said their countries were ready to sign treaty within year if sanctions against them were lifted, was optimistic in US; maintains that Sharif and Vajpayee face politically insecure futures and would pay high price with voters if they signed treaty without bringing home goods (M)

1998-09-30.title: New Peace Diplomacy Gets Mostly Shrugs in Israel
1998-09-30.abstract: There are neither cheers nor boos in Israel following Pres Clinton's intervention in stalemated peace talks--indication that few believe there has been diplomatic breakthrough worth celebrating or condemning; many Israelis debate Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu's intentions and political repercussions for him if peace agreement is reached during next round of three-way talks (M)

1998-09-30.title: Clinton Presses Terrorism Issue in 2d Meeting With Arafat
1998-09-30.abstract: Pres Clinton meets with Yasir Arafat and emphasizes work that Palestinians need to do to combat terrorism as Israelis withdraw from additional 13 percent of West Bank; Clinton's aides say he is determined to complete interim agreement between Israel and Palestinians in few weeks; car apparently being equipped as bomb explodes in Ramallah, killing Hamas participant; photo (M)

1998-09-30.title: Massacres by Serbian Forces in 3 Kosovo Villages
1998-09-30.abstract: Ethnic Albanians in Gornji Obrinje and neighboring villages bury 34 people who were killed on Sept 26, two days after villages came under siege by Yugoslav Army and Serbian forces; killings appear to show that forces of Yugoslav President, Slobodan Milosevic, have been conducting campaign of terror and destruction against ethnic Albanian civilians in Kosovo that is intended to intimidate them, but which appears instead to be inspiring even stronger defiance; map; photos (M)
